name: Round Rock Independent School District
districtFact: Round Rock ISD serves the northern Austin suburbs in Williamson County — home to Dell Technologies' global headquarters — and formally observes Diwali as a district-wide school holiday, placing it among a small number of US public school districts to do so.

about.title: About Round Rock Independent School District
about.content: One thing that catches newcomers off guard: a Cedar Park, Hutto, or Pflugerville mailing address doesn't automatically mean those school districts. Portions of RRISD territory carry those zip codes while falling entirely within Round Rock ISD. The district serves Round Rock and parts of Cedar Park — with Brushy Creek and Avery Ranch making up much of the recent residential growth — and draws a large international workforce from the technology employers concentrated in the corridor.

about.title: What to Know Before the First School Year
about.content: Round Rock ISD formally observes Diwali as a school holiday — the calendar labels it "Diwali / Student and Staff Holiday." Because Diwali follows the Hindu lunar calendar, the date changes every year; it typically falls in late October. Check the official RRISD calendar each fall rather than using last year's date. The district also observes Indigenous Peoples' Day. One structural difference from most Texas districts: the calendar is built around 170–171 instructional days achieved through extended daily minutes — fewer calendar days than the 175 or 180 most Texas districts schedule. The first semester ends before Winter Break, so students return in January without the mid-semester transition common in other Texas districts.

content: Which high school students attend in Round Rock ISD depends on home address, not neighborhood name. In the Brushy Creek and Avery Ranch corridors, adjacent streets can fall in different high school zones — Westwood, Round Rock, McNeil, and Cedar Ridge each serve distinct parts of the district. Don't assume a neighborhood's name tells you which campus — confirm through the address lookup at roundrockisd.org.

Round Rock ISD Spring Break typically falls in the second or third week of March — one week later than Northside ISD in San Antonio. If you're coordinating travel with relatives in other Texas metros, download both calendars rather than assuming they align.

The 2026–2027 school year in Round Rock ISD starts August 19 — one of the later start dates among major Texas districts that year. Families arriving from districts that start in early August have more scheduling flexibility during the transition summer.

districtFaqs.q: Does Round Rock ISD observe Diwali as a school holiday?
districtFaqs.a: Round Rock ISD observes Diwali as a district-wide school holiday for students and staff — it appears on the official calendar as "Diwali / Student and Staff Holiday." The date changes each year because Diwali follows the Hindu lunar calendar. In 2026–2027, it falls on October 19. Check the official RRISD calendar each fall for the current year's date.

districtFaqs.q: When does Round Rock ISD Spring Break fall?
districtFaqs.a: Round Rock ISD Spring Break typically falls in the second or third week of March. For 2025–2026, Spring Break ran March 16–20; for 2026–2027, it runs March 15–19. This places RRISD's Spring Break one week later than Northside ISD in San Antonio and generally near Austin ISD's timing. Households coordinating travel with relatives in other Texas metros should check each district's specific dates, as they don't always line up.

districtFaqs.q: When does the Round Rock ISD school year start?
districtFaqs.a: Round Rock ISD's start date varies by year. The 2025–2026 school year began August 12, 2025; the 2026–2027 school year begins August 19, 2026 — one of the later start dates among large Texas districts that year. The first semester ends before Winter Break; students don't return from the break to begin a new semester. Check the official calendar at roundrockisd.org for current-year dates.

districtFaqs.q: How does Round Rock ISD compare to Austin ISD?
districtFaqs.a: Round Rock ISD and Austin ISD are separate districts. Austin ISD serves the city of Austin; Round Rock ISD serves the northern suburbs in Williamson County. Their school calendars are broadly similar — both start in August and observe the same major holidays — but Spring Break timing may differ by a day or a week in a given year, and Round Rock ISD observes Diwali as a school holiday, which Austin ISD doesn't. Parents with children split between the two districts should download both calendars.

districtFaqs.q: How many instructional days does Round Rock ISD have?
districtFaqs.a: Parents moving from other Texas districts often notice that RRISD shows fewer instructional days on the calendar — 170 to 171, versus the 175 or 180 most Texas districts schedule. The difference comes from longer school days that meet state requirements without the higher day count. In practice, the calendar builds in enough buffer to absorb most weather closures without end-of-year makeup days.

districtFaqs.q: How do I find my child's school in Round Rock ISD?
districtFaqs.a: School assignment in Round Rock ISD is based on your home address and current attendance zone boundaries. The district's website at roundrockisd.org includes an address lookup tool that identifies your assigned elementary, middle, and high school. Because the Brushy Creek, Avery Ranch, and northern growth corridors have seen ongoing development, zone boundaries shift when new campuses open. Households in recently built subdivisions — particularly those with Cedar Park or Hutto mailing addresses that fall within RRISD territory — should verify their assignment through the district rather than relying on builder materials or neighborhood listings.

livingHere.intro: Round Rock ISD spans an unusual mix — established neighborhoods near downtown Round Rock, newer master-planned communities in Brushy Creek and Avery Ranch, and subdivisions with Cedar Park addresses that are still RRISD campuses. Spring Break runs in the second or third week of March, a week later than San Antonio-area districts — a gap that surprises families coordinating with relatives in South Texas.

highlights.label: Dell Technologies and Tech Sector Relocations
highlights.detail: Dell Technologies' global headquarters is in Round Rock, and the broader tech corridor has attracted a large international workforce to the area. The district handles a consistent volume of mid-year enrollments from corporate relocations. For families arriving from outside Texas, credit evaluation at the high school level is handled by the receiving campus — reaching out to Westwood, Round Rock High, or whichever campus is the destination before the move is faster than starting the process on the first day.

highlights.label: High School Attendance Zones
highlights.detail: Round Rock ISD operates multiple high schools — Westwood, Round Rock, McNeil, Cedar Ridge, and Stony Point each serve different parts of the district. In the Brushy Creek, Avery Ranch, and Cedar Park fringe areas, home address determines which high school a student attends — neighborhood name and ZIP code don't. Adjacent streets in a subdivision can feed different high schools when zone lines run through the area. Builder marketing materials haven't always matched current zones; confirm at roundrockisd.org.

highlights.label: Brushy Creek Corridor and Zone Assignment
highlights.detail: The Brushy Creek area — stretching from the 620/183A interchange northwest through Avery Ranch — is one of the fastest-growing residential zones in the Austin metro. Round Rock ISD has added campuses here repeatedly over the past decade, and each opening redraws the surrounding attendance zones. Families moving into recently built sections have sometimes found their assigned elementary school changed between the time they signed a contract and when enrollment opened. Two homes on opposite sides of the same street in Avery Ranch can be assigned to different elementary campuses when a new school opens nearby. The district's address lookup at roundrockisd.org reflects current boundaries.

highlights.label: August Heat
highlights.detail: School starts in mid-August — in most years, during the hottest stretch of the Central Texas summer. Daily highs regularly exceed 100°F in August, and the heat index adds further to that. Outdoor PE schedules are modified during heat advisories, and after-school athletics typically run in early morning or late afternoon to avoid peak heat.

highlights.label: Weather Closures
highlights.detail: Winter weather closures in Round Rock are more likely to come from ice than snow — modest precipitation can still freeze overpasses and ground traffic for a full day. The 170–171-day calendar builds in buffer to absorb most closures without makeup days; late spring flooding occasionally disrupts afternoon dismissal as well.

relatedDistricts.name: Austin Independent School District
relatedDistricts.reason: Austin ISD serves the city of Austin immediately south of Round Rock ISD; their Spring Break dates generally align but are not always identical, and Austin ISD doesn't observe Diwali as a school holiday.

relatedDistricts.name: Leander Independent School District
relatedDistricts.reason: Leander ISD serves Cedar Park and the communities west of Round Rock ISD's territory; households near the boundary between the two districts should confirm which district their address falls in, as both serve parts of the Cedar Park area.

relatedDistricts.name: Pflugerville Independent School District
relatedDistricts.reason: Pflugerville ISD serves the eastern Austin suburbs adjacent to Round Rock ISD; both districts draw from the technology-sector workforce in the Austin corridor, and Spring Break dates between the two may or may not align in a given year.

sources.label: NCES — Common Core of Data, Round Rock ISD
